agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Race condition between stock check and decrement will oversell inventory under concurrent requests"
  },
  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "TOCTOU race condition: stock check and decrement are not atomic",
      "risk": "Two or more concurrent requests read the same stock value, both pass the check, both decrement — resulting in negative stock and overselling inventory. With popular products and a public API, this is near-certain under real traffic.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart function, lines 5-9 (getStock → check → addItem → decrementStock)",
      "reproduction": "1. Product X has stock=1. 2. User A calls addToCart(A, X, 1) — reads stock=1, passes check. 3. User B calls addToCart(B, X, 1) — reads stock=1 before A's decrement, passes check. 4. Both addItem and decrementStock execute — stock goes to -1, two users 'own' the same unit.",
      "fix": "Replace the read-then-check-then-write with an atomic conditional decrement: `UPDATE products SET stock = stock - $qty WHERE id = $productId AND stock >= $qty`. Check affected rows — if 0, throw insufficient stock. This moves the guard into the database where row-level locking prevents the race. Alternatively, use SELECT ... FOR UPDATE if you need the two-step flow.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Stock integrity, order accuracy, revenue correctness"
    },
    {
      "title": "Non-atomic cart add + stock decrement leaves inconsistent state on partial failure",
      "risk": "If addItem succeeds but decrementStock fails (DB timeout, connection drop, constraint error), the item is in the user's cart but stock was never reduced. Other users can also claim that stock. Conversely, if addItem fails after stock is decremented (reversed order), stock is lost with no cart entry.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart function, lines 8-9 (addItem then decrementStock)",
      "reproduction": "1. addItem succeeds for user A. 2. decrementStock throws (e.g., network timeout). 3. Cart now contains an item that stock still reflects as available. 4. Another user purchases the same unit — double allocation.",
      "fix": "Wrap both operations in a database transaction: BEGIN → atomic decrement (with stock >= qty guard) → addItem → COMMIT. On any failure the entire operation rolls back, guaranteeing consistency.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes (same fix as finding 1 — transaction wrapping)",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data consistency, inventory accuracy"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on public API parameters",
      "risk": "quantity=0 or negative values bypass the stock check (stock < 0 is always false for positive stock) and could corrupt inventory counts (decrementing by a negative number increments stock). A malicious caller can mint infinite stock.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "addToCart function, entry point — no validation before line 5",
      "reproduction": "Call addToCart('user1', 'prod1', -100). Stock check: `stock < -100` → false for any non-negative stock. decrementStock subtracts -100, effectively adding 100 units to inventory.",
      "fix": "Add guard at function entry: `if (!Number.isInteger(quantity) || quantity <= 0) throw new Error('Invalid quantity');` Also validate userId and productId are non-empty strings.",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Security, inventory integrity"
    }
  ],
  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No idempotency protection",
      "description": "If a client retries a failed/timed-out request, the same item could be added to the cart multiple times and stock decremented repeatedly. A public API should use an idempotency key to prevent duplicate processing.",
      "location": "addToCart function signature — no idempotency key parameter"
    }
  ],
  "suggestions": [
    "Consider a rate limiter per userId to mitigate bot-driven stock exhaustion attacks",
    "Add observability: emit metrics on stock contention (failed atomic decrements) to detect hot products early"
  ],
  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "Current code will oversell inventory immediately under concurrent traffic — this is not a theoretical risk, it is a guaranteed production failure for any popular product",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — standalone function, rollback is trivial if not yet deployed",
    "backward_compatibility": "Fix changes only internal implementation; API contract (params, return, thrown error) stays the same",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging or metrics — failures will be silent until inventory reconciliation reveals negative stock",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": []
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Do not deploy until race condition and transaction issues are fixed"
  },
  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "architectural_concern",
      "description": "The stock management pattern needs an atomic decrement-or-fail primitive at the data layer, and all cart+inventory mutations must be wrapped in a transaction. This is a data integrity architecture decision, not a simple bug fix — it determines whether the system uses optimistic locking, pessimistic locking (SELECT FOR UPDATE), or atomic conditional updates, and whether the transaction boundary lives in the service layer or a stored procedure.",
      "routed_to": "MJ",
      "blocked_criteria": ["Stock integrity under concurrent access", "Cart-inventory consistency guarantee"],
      "recommendation": "MJ should define the concurrency control pattern (atomic conditional UPDATE vs. SELECT FOR UPDATE vs. distributed lock) and transaction boundary strategy before Shaq implements the fix"
    }
  ],
  "confidence": {
    "level": 72,
    "high_confidence_areas": ["Race condition is certain under concurrency", "Partial failure leaves inconsistent state", "Negative quantity bypasses guard"],
    "low_confidence_areas": ["Unknown whether db abstraction layer already supports transactions", "Unknown if decrementStock handles negative values at the DB level"],
    "assumptions": ["db.products.decrementStock performs a simple subtraction without its own concurrency guard", "No external transaction wrapper exists around this function", "This is the actual production code path, not wrapped by middleware that validates input"]
  }
}

content.thinking: Classic TOCTOU race condition in cart stock management.
